video_title: Guy Goes Berserk for Biscuits & Gravy in Portland 🤤 | Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives | Food Network

restaurants.guy_intro: About three minutes on biscuits. This is the perfect bite. Biscuit and huckleberry jam. And a biscuit with sausage gravy. Do you run here? Some good home-cooked comfort food. People needed a place they could stop by and get a good hearty breakfast. Like this North Carolina classic. The best biscuits and gravy. So get me started.

dishes.description: Hand-built biscuits made with all-purpose unbleached flour, baking powder, baking soda, salt, buttermilk, full-fat yogurt, eggs, and shredded frozen butter, folded for layers, baked just below 400 degrees for 15 minutes.
dishes.guy_response: That is some of the best texture in a biscuit I've ever had. That is the best. The flakiness is light, but there's real substance to it. But then again, I could be eating a paper towel with that gravy on it. Wow. Pillar. I'd say these are some of the best biscuits I've ever had. Flaky and delicious.

dishes.description: Made with olive oil, pureed onions, ground pork, water, flour, sausage spice mix (cayenne, salt, pepper, nutmeg, coriander, thyme, sage), heavy cream, whole milk, buttered flour roux, maple syrup, chives, and paprika.
dishes.guy_response: That is some of the best texture in a biscuit I've ever had. That is the best. The flakiness is light, but there's real substance to it. But then again, I could be eating a paper towel with that gravy on it. Wow. Pillar. I'd say these are some of the best biscuits I've ever had. Flaky and delicious.

dishes.description: A giant sandwich featuring a biscuit, buttermilk-brined fried chicken (soaked 24 hours, dredged in self-rising biscuit flour, coarse ground pepper, egg wash with hot sauce, seasoned with garlic salt, salt, pepper, Cajun seasoning), bacon, sausage gravy, and cheddar cheese.
dishes.guy_response: That's ridiculous. Everybody comes together. That's right. I love it. It's got everything on it. I had my Reggie and then most of my wife's Reggie. This place is great. It's not just bacon and eggs and hash browns. It's something really unique.

restaurants.guy_intro: Biscuit time. So we're going to start with AP flour, sugar, baking powder, salt. Mix the dry ingredients. Add the butter. Buttermilk. Okay. Are we just pinching them off on the side like this? Absolutely. Oven. 350, about 30 minutes. We brush them at the very end with melted butter. What are we making next? Red pepper jelly.

dishes.description: Made with AP flour, sugar, baking powder, salt, butter, and buttermilk, pinched off and baked at 350 degrees for 30 minutes, brushed with melted butter at the end.
Made with roasted red peppers, sugar, apple cider vinegar, butter, and pectin, brought to a boil and cooled.

Chicken brined for two days in a sweet tea brine (sugar, salt, hot water, garlic, black pepper, lemons, bay leaves, parsley, iced tea), then battered in day-old pancake batter and a flour dredge, cooked low and slow.
dishes.guy_response: But boy, the crunch on that chicken. That batter, really tasty.

dishes.description: A biscuit topped with country sausage gravy (oil blend, butter, onions, chorizo, country sausage mix, cayenne, sage, heavy cream, culinary cream, whole milk, green onion, black pepper, kosher salt, dried sage), applewood smoked bacon, and fried chicken (buttermilk, hot sauce, salt, pepper, AP flour, coarse ground pepper, white pepper, garlic powder, canola oil, topped with pepper jack and mustard cheese, broiled).
dishes.guy_response: I can't say I'm that much of a biscuit aficionado. But this is nice and light and fluffy. Nice crust on top. Here we go. So the gravy is not really big and heavy. It's a lot lighter than I thought it would be. When you come to North Carolina and you think that you're going to have a gravy, bacon, chicken, cheese, biscuit meal, that's the kind of dish you fall asleep in.

restaurants.guy_intro: Honey butter chicken biscuit, table three. We're making the biscuits. Yes, sir. Love it. We freeze our grated butter and our flour. Everything stays really cold, comes out better that way. When the butter starts to melt inside of the flour, it creates its own little level. That is the key.

dishes.description: Made with AP flour, sugar, baking powder, salt, pepper, buttermilk, and sour cream, with grated frozen butter, baked at 350 degrees for 20 minutes.
dishes.guy_response: Love a savory biscuit. Who put that much black pepper in a biscuit? You better mean it. And you mean it.

dishes.description: Honey and butter infused with mushroom powder, chili crisp, and pickled jalapeno liquid.
dishes.guy_response: The honey butter crisp is redonk-a-donk.

dishes.description: Chicken brined in pickle brine for about an hour, then soaked in buttermilk, dredged in AP flour, potato flour, salt, gochugaru, bullet chilies, garlic powder, and black pepper, then fried.
dishes.guy_response: The chicken is delicious. Great crunch.

dishes.description: A biscuit topped with American cheese, pickle brined fried chicken, and brushed with honey chili crisp butter.
dishes.guy_response: Dynamite. The chicken is delicious. Great crunch. The honey butter crisp is redonk-a-donk. Love a savory biscuit. Who put that much black pepper in a biscuit? You better mean it. And you mean it. That's like an exceptional chicken in biscuit.

restaurants.guy_intro: We're going to do chicken and biscuits? Yeah. We're just going to start off with the brine, hot water, garlic powder, kosher salt, some sugar. Throw in an ice. Cool that down so we're not scorching the chicken. Chicken breast, chicken thigh. We do an airliner that we cut the wing off of.

dishes.description: Chicken brined overnight in hot water, garlic powder, kosher salt, sugar, then cryovac bagged and sous vide at 165 degrees for about an hour to par-cook, then dredged in rice flour and fried to order.
dishes.guy_response: Great chicken. This is tender. Great flavor. You're definitely getting a little bit of the brine. It's a light, crisp fry.

dishes.description: Biscuits made with flour, salt, baking soda, baking powder, cold butter, local goat cheddar, green onion, and buttermilk, folded for layers, baked at 350 degrees for about 18 minutes.
A toasted house-made biscuit with horseradish crema, sous vide fried chicken breast, house-made pickles, and rice flour onion rings.
dishes.guy_response: Nice tender biscuit. Great chicken. This is tender. Great flavor. You're definitely getting a little bit of the brine. It's a light, crisp fry. And the brine of the pickle and the horseradish sauce is dynamite. I give you props.
